question: Should the first Genesis-Core-V2 seed be runtime-only or runtime-plus-API, and what dependency/bootstrap order is minimally required to open that seed honestly?

operational_fixture_needed: data/** selection for first reproducible runtime execution

api_service_shell: service-edge dependencies such as Bitfinex-facing API paths
paper_live_adjacent: exchange/service edges not needed for runtime kernel

key_observations: The runtime seed candidate imports into backtest, strategy, indicators, regime, config-support, utility, and observability families without requiring server.py or src/core/api/**.
src/core/server.py is an app assembler that imports API routers and service edges, making it additive to the runtime kernel rather than a prerequisite for it.
ChampionLoader hard-requires config.timeframe_configs at import time, while config/models/registry.json and champion payloads remain optional or fallback-backed.
tests/integration/test_config_endpoints.py is API-phase coverage because it imports core.server and FastAPI TestClient, while the governance tests align with a runtime-only first seed.
unverified: The exact minimum package metadata shape for a future V2 repo.
The smallest reproducible data fixture that should travel with a runtime-only seed.
Whether any current config/models payload is fresh enough to carry unchanged.
Whether any current config/strategy/champions payload is fresh enough to carry unchanged.
Whether paper/live surfaces should ever be part of the first V2 remit after the API shell arrives.

phase_plan.title: open runtime-only seed
phase_plan.goal: seed the active runtime kernel plus required support families and runtime-only guardrails

phase_plan.title: add minimal bootstrap shell
phase_plan.goal: define minimal package metadata, README/bootstrap note, defaults policy, and first reproducible data/input fixture

phase_plan.title: admit stateful artifacts one bucket at a time
phase_plan.goal: decide models, champions, and future runtime-state payloads explicitly instead of blindly cloning them

phase_plan.title: add API service shell
phase_plan.goal: bring in server.py, src/core/api/**, and config endpoint integration coverage only after runtime kernel and bootstrap stabilize

phase_plan.title: consider paper-live expansion last
phase_plan.goal: keep operational and exchange-facing edges out of the initial seed unless still explicitly desired

what_changed: V2 planning now has one concrete execution order instead of only boundary buckets.
The first-seed decision is now explicit: runtime-only, not runtime-plus-API.
Runtime-only guardrails are separated from API-phase integration coverage.
Bootstrap work is split into hard-bootstrap, soft-bootstrap, and state-admission phases.
what_did_not_change: No files were copied or moved.
No current branch state was promoted as future V2 default truth.
Locally modified unrelated files remained untouched.
